programme.title: PL STORIES EP15: TIM CAHILL
programme.sub-title: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.
programme.desc: With over 200 appearances for Everton, Tim Cahill established himself as the high-energy midfielder who helped drive the side. For PL Stories this week, Cahill talks through his journey to the Premier League from battling his way to make it as a footballer in his native Australia, leaving home as a teenager and adapting to English life, scoring in a World Cup as well as the difficult decision to leave Goodison Park after 8 years.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP29: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP18: DYLAN TOMBIDES
programme.sub-title: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.
programme.desc: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP19: BASSEY x IWOBI
programme.sub-title: Fulham’s Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have been instrumental in the club’s resurgence. Off the field, they’ve shared similar experiences playing for Nigeria, their charity work in the community, as well as gaining an online following for their comical social media clips. In this week’s Big Interview, the pair sit down to talk in further depth about their friendship, their backstories, the importance of their Nigerian heritage and how Marco Silva has helped turn Fulham into one of the Premier League’s toughest opponents.
programme.desc: Fulham’s Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have been instrumental in the club’s resurgence. programme.title: PL STORIES DNTX EP23: SPORTS SCIENCE
programme.sub-title: We explore the cutting-edge world of sports science and its transformative impact on Premier League football. From player fitness and injury prevention to nutrition and recovery, the show uncovers how technology and data-driven insights are revolutionizing the game and discover how these innovations are shaping the future of football and giving teams the edge in the world’s most competitive league.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: MARK VIDUKA
programme.sub-title: Australian striker Mark Viduka was at Leeds United at one of the most iconic moments in the clubs history and also one of their worst! We sit down with the retired Socceroo to get a brutally honest appraisal of what went on behind the scenes at Elland Road at the height of their push for Premier League and European success and find out who he thought was really to blame for the club’s downfall in the early noughties.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP25: (Subbed) HERNAN CRESPO
programme.sub-title: We delve into the remarkable journey of Argentine football legend Hernán Crespo. From his early days as a striker with clubs like Parma, Lazio, and Chelsea, to his evolution into a title winning coach, Crespo's career has been filled with highs and challenges. We follow his transition from player to manager and join him at Al Ain, where we get a look into how he navigates the pressures and ambitions of managing one of the UAE's top football clubs.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP19: BASSEY x IWOBI
programme.sub-title: Fulham’s Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have been instrumental in the club’s resurgence. Off the field, they’ve shared similar experiences playing for Nigeria, their charity work in the community, as well as gaining an online following for their comical social media clips. In this week’s Big Interview, the pair sit down to talk in further depth about their friendship, their backstories, the importance of their Nigerian heritage and how Marco Silva has helped turn Fulham into one of the Premier League’s toughest opponents.
programme.desc: Fulham’s Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have been instrumental in the club’s resurgence. programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP10: MICHAEL OWEN: MY BREAKTHROUGH YEARS
programme.sub-title: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. Owen recalls those campaigns for the Reds, reliving the key moments that helped him develop into one of the greatest players of his generation.
programme.desc: Michael Owen burst onto the scene for Liverpool at just seventeen years of age, and in two consecutive seasons the teenage sensation won the Premier League Golden Boot. programme.title: PL REWIND EP08: LEON OSMAN: MY EVERTON STORY
programme.sub-title: Leon Osman recalls his favourite moments in an Everton shirt, from imitating a Premier League legend at White Hart Lane, to scoring in his 400th appearance for the Toffees.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP24: (Subbed) SAMI HYPPIA
programme.sub-title: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.
programme.desc: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP33: (Subbed) EIDUR GUDJOHNSEN
programme.sub-title: PL Stories heads to Iceland to meet the first, and only Icelandic Premier League, winner Eidur Gudjohnsen. With the help of his father Arnor, as well as friends Gudni Bergsson and Lárus Sigurðsson, Gudjohnsen reflects on the origins of a decorated career which was almost derailed by a devastating leg break.
programme.desc: PL Stories heads to Iceland to meet the first, and only Icelandic Premier League, winner Eidur Gudjohnsen. With the help of his father Arnor, as well as friends Gudni Bergsson and Lárus Sigurðsson, Gudjohnsen reflects on the origins of a decorated career which was almost derailed by a devastating leg break.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP24: (Subbed) SAMI HYPPIA
programme.sub-title: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.
programme.desc: From his early days in Finland to lifting the Champions League trophy in 2005, we chart Sami Hyypiä’s journey to the top of English football. We join him for a snowmobiling adventure across the icy wilderness of Finland and though interviews and personal moments, showcase not only his leadership and skill on the pitch but also his deep connection to his roots.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP33: (Subbed) EIDUR GUDJOHNSEN
programme.sub-title: PL Stories heads to Iceland to meet the first, and only Icelandic Premier League, winner Eidur Gudjohnsen. With the help of his father Arnor, as well as friends Gudni Bergsson and Lárus Sigurðsson, Gudjohnsen reflects on the origins of a decorated career which was almost derailed by a devastating leg break.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP30: (Subbed) BASQUE MANAGERS
programme.sub-title: We explore the remarkable impact of the Basque region on the Premier League, focusing on the careers of the four current Basque managers who are leaving their mark in England’s topflight. From tactical brilliance to relentless passion, this program delves into why the Basque Country has produced such a high number of successful managers and how their unique style has left a lasting legacy in the Premier League.
